question_text: Which of the following would NOT cause a sound wave's amplitude to increase?

options.label: B
options.text: Increasing the frequency of a sound wave

options.label: C
options.text: Turning up the volume on a speaker

options.label: D
options.text: Plucking a guitar string with greater force

explanation: Amplitude is the maximum displacement of particles as a wave passes and controls loudness. Increasing frequency (how often particles vibrate) does not make the vibrations bigger; it just makes them happen more often. Amplitude and frequency are independent properties.
